game_meta.name: Rocket League
game_meta.tagline: Vehicular soccer — 1v1/2v2/3v3 rocket-powered cars hitting a giant ball into a goal. Mechanics-driven.

sides.attack: Pushing toward enemy goal, attempting to score
sides.defense: Backboard / net defense, denying the shot

game_meta.character_vocab: cars (cosmetic — Octane / Dominus / Fennec / etc. are functionally identical in modern RL)
game_meta.round_economy: No buy economy — boost is collected on field (small pads = 12, large pads = 100). 100 boost is the cap.
game_meta.round_format: 5-minute game (or first to 1 in overtime). MMR-based ranking from Bronze → SSL.
key_mechanics: Ball cam: locks camera to the ball — should be on 95%+ of match (toggle off only for boost grabbing)
Speedflip: fastest kickoff method, diagonal flip + air roll to flatten car for supersonic motion
Half-flip: reverse-drive + back diagonal flip + cancel = backwards-to-forward in 1.2s
Wave-dash: dodge while landing for instant forward momentum
Aerial: jump + boost + pitch up — basic mid-air play
Fast aerial: jump → immediately boost while pitching back (under 0.1s transition)
Power-slide: slide for sharp turns without losing speed
Flip reset: touch ball with all 4 wheels mid-air for fresh flip (Champion+ mechanic)
Musty flick: upside-down dodge flick for unpredictable shot (SSL signature)
Air dribble: stick ball to roof mid-air, fly with possession (GC+ mechanic)
Boost economy: never go below 30 boost on engagements, curve through small pads
Rotation: clockwise/counter-clockwise cycle through first/second/third man positions
match_types: Snow Day: 3v3 hockey variant with puck instead of ball
Rumble: 3v3 with power-ups (boot, tornado, magnet, etc.)

phases.name: Kickoff
phases.key_play: Speedflip kickoff for fastest contact. Decide kickoff type before whistle: speedflip, fake flip cancel, or diagonal.
phases.rotation_role: Position 1: kickoff player. Position 2: mid-cheat. Position 3: full back.

phases.name: Possession
phases.key_play: Hold ball on hood (dribble) or 50/50 contest. Pass to teammate for two-touch setup.
phases.rotation_role: Cycle clockwise. First man hits, rotates to last man. Second becomes first. Last becomes second.

phases.name: Transition
phases.key_play: After 50/50 contest, read ball direction. If it goes to enemy, fall back to defense. If yours, push forward.
phases.rotation_role: Adapt rotation: if pushed back, regroup at back wall. If forward, set up shot.

phases.name: Defense
phases.key_play: Backboard read: anticipate rebound bounce. Hold far post on cross-net shots. No panic clears.
phases.rotation_role: Last man on back post. Second man as backup. First man challenges ball.

phases.name: Kickoff
phases.key_play: Speedflip kickoff. One player cheats forward, one falls back for follow-up.
phases.rotation_role: Position 1: kickoff. Position 2: full back.

phases.name: Possession
phases.key_play: 2v2 is faster — possession is brief. Look for fast pass or shot, not long dribbles.
phases.rotation_role: One-back rotation: if teammate goes, you're last man.

phases.name: Defense
phases.key_play: One-back rotation: if teammate is committed forward, you're full back. Anticipate enemy clear.
phases.rotation_role: Always have one man back. Never both forward.

phases.name: Kickoff
phases.key_play: Speedflip kickoff is critical. Win the kickoff = ~70% chance to win that round.
phases.rotation_role: 1v1: no rotation. Hit and recover.

phases.name: Recovery / Boost
phases.key_play: Half-flip back to net after every touch. Grab corner 100 boost on the way back.
phases.rotation_role: 1v1: solo recovery. Half-flip or wave-dash for fast back-to-goal.

phases.name: Defense
phases.key_play: Backboard read: anticipate rebound. Goalie position on back post, not center net.
phases.rotation_role: 1v1: hold back post for cross-shot defense, drive forward to challenge.

phases.name: Series start
phases.key_play: Game 1 = scouting. Read opponent's kickoff style + rotation tendencies. Don't burn ult plays game 1.
phases.rotation_role: Standard 3v3 rotation.

phases.name: Comp adjust
phases.key_play: Between games: adjust based on opponent reads. Counter their rotation patterns.
phases.rotation_role: Switch positions if comp adjustment requires (defender becomes mid, etc.).

phases.name: Closeout
phases.key_play: Match-ending defense: pre-position for opponent's last-shot attempt. No risk plays. Run clock.
phases.rotation_role: Conservative rotation — always 2 back when leading.

phases.name: Dunk setup
phases.key_play: Wall pop the ball into aerial position. Hold ball above rim for dunk angle.
phases.rotation_role: One player aerials, one waits at floor for rebound.

phases.name: Net defense
phases.key_play: Below-rim hold: defenders stay below rim level. Block dunks by jumping at last second.
phases.rotation_role: Both defenders at rim height — one challenges, one rebounds.

phases.name: Face-off
phases.key_play: Center puck kickoff. Puck slides further than ball — speed flip wins contest.
phases.rotation_role: Standard kickoff but adapted for puck physics.

phases.name: Possession
phases.key_play: Puck slides on the ice — quick passes for offensive setup.
phases.rotation_role: Standard 3v3 rotation with hockey physics.

phases.name: Defense
phases.key_play: Net + crease defense. Block low-angle shots — puck slides fast.
phases.rotation_role: Crease defender + back-up.

phases.name: Opening the floor
phases.key_play: Build damage on tiles with ball touches. Damaged tiles break into goal pit.
phases.rotation_role: Attackers focus on damage tiles, defenders mend / cover broken tiles.

phases.name: Breaking tiles
phases.key_play: Charge ball for power shot (yellow charge), break tile cluster into goal pit.
phases.rotation_role: Power-shot player + cover.

phases.name: Defense
phases.key_play: Cover broken tiles, mend with low-power touches.
phases.rotation_role: Tile defenders + ball intercept.

phases.name: Power-up advantage
phases.key_play: Got the item: use it offensively or defensively based on context. Boot for enemy, Tornado for chaos, Magnet for ball control.
phases.rotation_role: Item user gets priority; teammates set up for follow-up.

phases.name: Scramble
phases.key_play: Item used / waiting for next: anticipate enemy item use, play safe rotations.
phases.rotation_role: Standard rotation with item awareness.

First Man.description: Closest to the ball, attacking it. Takes the touch, then rotates back to last man.
common_mistakes: Hitting ball without aim direction (random touch)
Going for a 50/50 with no boost
No directional touch (ball goes to enemy)
First Man.ideal_use: Take the contested touch, aim it toward goal or open midfield, rotate to last man.

Second Man.description: Midfield, ready to follow up if first man misses or wins the 50/50.
common_mistakes: Not committing on first man's miss (lost momentum)
Second Man.ideal_use: Cover the follow-up after first man, take the second touch, rotate forward.

Last Man / Defender.description: Defends the goal, on the back wall. Last line of defense.
common_mistakes: Pushing forward off the back wall (out of position for defense)
Panic-clearing every rebound (gives ball back to enemy)
Last Man / Defender.ideal_use: Hold back post, anticipate rebounds, no panic clears — make directional clears.

bronze: Ball cam off most of the match
Chasing ball instead of reading it
silver: Rotating through center of field (wrong direction)
Going into challenges with under 30 boost
No quick chat — team has no comm
Holding ball cam off too long
gold: Braking to turn instead of power-sliding
Reacting to rebounds instead of anticipating
platinum: No hood dribble — every touch back to opponent
diamond: Reacting to shots instead of reading setups
gc_ssl: Risky plays when ahead in final minutes
Falling for fake challenges and aerials

positioning: Is ball cam on (95%+ of time)?
Is the player in correct rotation position (first man, second, last)?
Is the player following clockwise / counter-clockwise rotation?
Is the player on back post (defense) or mid-net (suboptimal)?
Is the player ahead of teammate during rotation (overcommit)?
Is the player in cover when defending (no exposure to cross-net)?
Is the player using high ground for shot setup?
mechanics: Is the player using power-slide on corners (not braking)?
Is the half-flip recovery clean and fast (1.2s)?
Is the wave-dash recovery used after aerials?
Is the player fast aerial transition under 0.1s?
Is yaw / pitch / roll used in aerials?
Is the player executing flip resets / musty flicks (Champion+)?
shot_quality: Is the player directional touch aimed at goal / open midfield?
Is the player using hood dribble for possession?
Is the player executing air dribbles (GC+)?
Is the player landing shots in upper 90 (corners)?
Is the player using shot fakes to draw enemy goalie?
Is the player setting up 2-touch passes with teammate?
Is the player using wall plays for unpredictable angles?
decisions: Did the player commit to ball or hesitate?
Did the player rotate back after touch?
Did the player challenge with boost or fall back?
Did the player take a 50/50 unwinnable?
Did the player anticipate the rebound or panic-clear?
Did the player double-commit with teammate?
Did the player track teammate boost levels?
Did the player call "going" / "rotate" on time?

vocabulary: Ball cam: Camera locked on the ball.
Free cam: Camera locked behind car (not ball).
Power-slide: Hold square/X for slide while turning.
Speedflip: Diagonal flip + air roll for fastest kickoff.
Half-flip: Reverse + back diagonal flip + cancel for fast back-to-front.
Wave-dash: Dodge while landing for forward momentum.
Aerial: Mid-air play.
Fast aerial: Jump → immediately boost while pitching back.
Flip: Double-tap jump for a dodge (8 directions).
Flip cancel: Pull stick opposite during flip to cancel it.
Flip reset: Touch ball with all 4 wheels mid-air for fresh flip.
Musty flick: Upside-down dodge flick for unpredictable shot.
Air dribble: Stick ball to roof mid-air, fly with possession.
Hood dribble: Hold ball on car's hood while driving.
Ceiling shot: Drive on ceiling, drop ball for surprise shot.
Wall play: Drive up wall for vertical entry.
Wall bounce: Off-wall recovery into shot.
Pinch: Two cars (or car + wall) compress ball for 2-3x speed.
Wall pinch: Hit ball into wall exactly when ball touches wall.
Corner pinch: Two teammates hit ball from opposite angles.
50/50: Contested ball where 2 cars both go for it.
Trade: Hitting ball back after enemy's touch.
Possession: Holding the ball with car (dribble / hood).
Rotation: Cycling between first / second / last man positions.
Clockwise rotation: Standard 3v3 rotation direction.
Counter-clockwise: Alternative rotation direction.
First man: Closest to ball.
Second man: Mid-position behind first.
Last man: Defender on back wall.
Double-commit: Two teammates going for ball at same time.
Boost economy: Managing boost across the match.
Big boost / 100 boost: Corner boost pad (full boost).
Small boost / 12 boost: Smaller pads (28 around the field).
Boost denial: Taking enemy's boost pads.
Mid-cheat: Forward position during kickoff for follow-up.
Full back: Back-wall position during kickoff.
Kickoff: Start of round after goal or start of game.
Fake flip cancel: Speedflip-look-alike kickoff that cancels.
Diagonal kickoff: Off-center kickoff from back-corner.
Speed kickoff: Straight-drive kickoff (no flip).
Boomer / Power shot: Hard rocket-powered shot.
Touch: Any contact between car and ball.
Directional touch: Touch aimed at specific direction (goal, open).
Demolition / Demo: Crashing into enemy car at supersonic speed (kills enemy).
Save: Blocking enemy shot.
Backboard: Back wall behind goal.
Backboard rebound: Ball bouncing off back wall.
Cross-net: Shot from one side of net to other.
Far post / Near post: Goal post sides.
Off-angle: Position enemy doesn't expect.
Reactive defense: Reacting after enemy shoots.
Anticipatory defense: Pre-positioning before enemy shoots.
Going / I got it: Quick chat — committing to ball.
Rotate: Quick chat — rotate back to position.
Defending: Quick chat — committing to defense.
Nice one: Positive quick chat (build morale).
What a save: Sarcastic quick chat (often toxic — be careful).
Supersonic: Maximum speed (2300 uu/s).
Air roll: Rotation around car's axis during aerial.
Yaw: Left/right rotation in air.
Pitch: Forward/back rotation (nose up/down).
Roll: Twist around car's axis.
BakkesMod: PC mod for training maps + custom features.
Workshop maps: Custom training maps via BakkesMod.
MMR: Matchmaking Rating, ranked metric.
